hero.headline: One Act of Kindness. A Million Ripples of Change.
hero.subheadline: InAmigos Foundation has touched 50,000+ lives across 28 states — feeding the hungry, educating the forgotten, empowering women, protecting animals, and greening the earth. Born in a small city, built for a big India.

about.tagline: From Bilaspur to 28 States — Rewriting India's Story, One Life at a Time.
about.paragraph1: InAmigos Foundation was born on September 23, 2020, in the heart of Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh — not in a boardroom, but in a moment of conviction. Founded by Mr. Govind Shukla, InAmigos began as a simple belief: that compassion, when organized, becomes unstoppable. Today, as a Section 8 registered non-profit licensed by the Central Government of India, we operate across 28 states with six transformative projects that span hunger relief, education, animal welfare, women's empowerment, environmental restoration, and youth skill development.
about.paragraph2: We are certified under 80G, 12A, CSR-1, registered with NITI Aayog, and hold the IAF ISO 9001:2015 quality management standard — because the communities we serve deserve nothing less than accountability, transparency, and excellence. Every rupee donated is tracked. Every meal served is counted. Every life changed is remembered.
about.paragraph3: With over 200 dedicated volunteers, 30,000+ trained interns, and a relentless drive to reach the last person in the last mile — InAmigos is not just a foundation. It is a movement. A family of change-makers who prove every single day that India's future is being built not by indifference, but by intention.

projects.name: SEVA
projects.tagline: No One Sleeps Hungry. No One Faces the Cold Alone.
projects.impact_statement: From railway platforms to remote villages, SEVA has distributed over 50,000 meals and essential clothing to India's most vulnerable — daily wage workers, homeless families, and disaster-affected communities. We believe that dignity begins with a full stomach and a warm blanket.

projects.name: BACHPANSHALA
projects.tagline: Every Child Deserves a Classroom — Even Under a Tree.
projects.impact_statement: BACHPANSHALA brings education and digital literacy to children who have been failed by the system — street children, first-generation learners, and kids in underserved communities. Through open-air classrooms, learning kits, and digital workshops, we are rewriting the futures that poverty tried to erase.

projects.name: JEEV
projects.tagline: Because Compassion Doesn't End at the Human Species.
projects.impact_statement: JEEV feeds and cares for 50+ stray animals every single day — dogs, cows, and birds who share our streets but rarely share our kindness. Through daily feeding drives, medical rescues, and community awareness, we are building a more humane India, one bowl at a time.

projects.name: UDAAN
projects.tagline: When She Rises, the Whole Nation Rises With Her.
projects.impact_statement: UDAAN empowers women through skill development workshops, menstrual hygiene awareness drives, self-help group collaborations, and livelihood training. From rural Chhattisgarh to urban centres, we are dismantling the barriers that keep women from realizing their full potential — one skill, one conversation, one opportunity at a time.
projects.key_stat: Thousands of women empowered across India

projects.name: PRAKRITI
projects.tagline: We Don't Inherit the Earth. We Plant It Forward.
projects.impact_statement: PRAKRITI has planted over 20,000 saplings across India and championed eco-friendly agricultural practices in farming communities. Through plantation drives, sustainability workshops, and green awareness campaigns, we are growing not just trees — but a generation that breathes responsibility.

projects.name: VIKAS
projects.tagline: Skills Today. Careers Tomorrow. Leaders Forever.
projects.impact_statement: VIKAS has trained over 30,000 interns in just four years — equipping young Indians with real-world skills in data analytics, finance, content creation, digital marketing, and social work. Our internship programs don't just build résumés; they build futures, confidence, and a generation ready to lead.

testimonials.name: Priya Sharma
testimonials.quote: I came from a village where girls were told to stop studying after 12th. InAmigos gave me an internship in digital marketing, and today I run social media campaigns for two startups. VIKAS didn't just teach me skills — it taught me I was capable of anything.

testimonials.name: Rajesh Verma
testimonials.quote: The first time I served meals at the railway station with SEVA, a man held my hand and cried. He hadn't eaten in two days. That moment changed me. I've been volunteering every weekend for three years now, and I've never looked back.

testimonials.name: Anita Kumari
testimonials.quote: Through the UDAAN program, I learned tailoring and joined a self-help group. Last month, I earned enough to pay my daughter's school fees on my own for the first time. InAmigos gave me more than a skill — they gave me my self-respect back.

testimonials.name: Mohammad Irfan
testimonials.quote: My son used to work at a tea stall. The BACHPANSHALA team convinced me to send him to their classes. Today he can read, write, and even use a computer. I never went to school, but my son will go to college — I'm sure of it now.

donate.headline: Your ₹500 Can Feed a Family for a Week.
donate.body: Every donation to InAmigos Foundation is tax-deductible under Section 80G. Whether it's ₹100 or ₹1,00,000 — every rupee is a vote for the India you want to see. Transparent. Accountable. Life-changing.

volunteer.headline: Lend Your Time. Change a Life. Transform Your Own.
volunteer.body: Join 200+ volunteers across 28 states who give their weekends, skills, and hearts to the communities that need them most. Whether you teach, cook, plant, or simply show up — you matter here.

partner.headline: CSR That Creates Real, Measurable Impact.
partner.body: We are CSR-1 certified, NITI Aayog registered, and ISO 9001:2015 compliant. Partner with InAmigos for your corporate social responsibility initiatives and see your investment transform into schools, meals, saplings, and smiles — with full documentation and impact reports.

events.title: World Water Day 2025 — Every Drop Counts
events.description: InAmigos volunteers organized water conservation awareness drives across multiple cities, educating communities on rainwater harvesting, reducing water waste, and protecting local water bodies. Hundreds of pledge cards were signed, and saplings were planted near water sources.

events.title: International Day of Happiness 2025 — Spreading Smiles
events.description: On the International Day of Happiness, InAmigos hosted community joy drives — distributing meals, organizing games for underprivileged children, and conducting gratitude workshops. Because happiness isn't a luxury; it's a right.

events.title: International Day of Women & Girls in Science 2025
events.description: InAmigos celebrated the brilliance of women in STEM through panel discussions, science workshops for young girls, and mentorship sessions with women scientists and engineers. UDAAN's mission is clear: no girl should ever be told that science isn't for her.

footer.tagline: Compassion is contagious. Start the epidemic.
